ddls:
CREATE TABLE "commands" (
  "command_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each command */,
  "command_text" TEXT /* Text of the command to be executed */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of what the command does */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the command was created */,
  "last_modified" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last modification to the command */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the command is currently active */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the admin who created the command */,
  "last_modified_by" INTEGER /* ID of the admin who last modified the command */,
  "tags" TEXT /* Tags for categorizing the command */,
  "requires_admin" INTEGER /* Indicates if the command requires admin execution */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("command_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "blocks" (
  "block_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each block type */,
  "block_name" TEXT /* Name of the block (e.g., cobblestone_wall) */,
  "is_waterlogged" INTEGER /* Indicates if the block is waterlogged */,
  "up_height" TEXT /* Height of the block when viewed from the up direction */,
  "north_height" TEXT /* Height of the block when viewed from the north direction */,
  "south_height" TEXT /* Height of the block when viewed from the south direction */,
  "west_height" TEXT /* Height of the block when viewed from the west direction */,
  "east_height" TEXT /* Height of the block when viewed from the east direction */,
  "block_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the block */,
  "block_texture" TEXT /* Texture path or identifier for the block */,
  "block_hardness" REAL /* Hardness of the block */,
  "is_transparent" INTEGER /* Indicates if the block is transparent */,
  "is_solid" INTEGER /* Indicates if the block is solid */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("block_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "score_conditions" (
  "condition_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each score condition */,
  "command_id" INTEGER /* ID of the command this condition relates to */,
  "score_type" TEXT /* Type of score to check (e.g., s.crhf.block_id) */,
  "score_value" INTEGER /* Value to compare against the player's score */,
  "operator" TEXT /* Operator for the condition (e.g., matches, less than) */,
  "min_value" INTEGER /* Minimum score value for the condition */,
  "max_value" INTEGER /* Maximum score value for the condition */,
  "is_range" INTEGER /* Indicates if the condition is a range */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("condition_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_score_conditions_command_id FOREIGN KEY ("command_id") REFERENCES commands ("command_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "admins" (
  "admin_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each admin */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the admin */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the admin */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the admin (e.g., superadmin, moderator) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the admin account is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the admin account was created */,
  "last_modified" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last modification to the admin account */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("admin_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "command_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "command_id" INTEGER /* ID of the executed command */,
  "admin_id" INTEGER /* ID of the admin who executed the command */,
  "execution_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the command was executed */,
  "execution_status" TEXT /* Status of the command execution (e.g., success, failure) */,
  "player_id" INTEGER /* ID of the player associated with the command execution */,
  "player_name" TEXT /* Name of the player associated with the command execution */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server where the command was executed */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the command was executed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_command_logs_command_id FOREIGN KEY ("command_id") REFERENCES commands ("command_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_command_logs_admin_id FOREIGN KEY ("admin_id") REFERENCES admins ("admin_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., viewer, editor) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* List of permissions associated with the role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the role was created */,
  "last_modified" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last modification to the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "role_assignments" (
  "assignment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role assignment */,
  "admin_id" INTEGER /* ID of the admin to whom the role is assigned */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role being assigned */,
  "assigned_by" INTEGER /* ID of the admin who assigned the role */,
  "assigned_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the role was assigned */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the role assignment is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("assignment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_role_assignments_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_role_assignments_admin_id FOREIGN KEY ("admin_id") REFERENCES admins ("admin_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "executions" (
  "execution_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each command execution */,
  "command_id" INTEGER /* ID of the command that was executed */,
  "parameters" TEXT /* Parameters used during the command execution */,
  "execution_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the command was executed */,
  "player_id" INTEGER /* ID of the player associated with the execution */,
  "player_name" TEXT /* Name of the player associated with the execution */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server where the execution occurred */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the execution was initiated */,
  "status_id" INTEGER /* ID of the status of the execution (foreign key to execution_status) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("execution_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_executions_status_id FOREIGN KEY ("status_id") REFERENCES execution_status ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_executions_command_id FOREIGN KEY ("command_id") REFERENCES commands ("command_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "execution_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status */,
  "status_description" TEXT /* Description of the status (e.g., success, failure) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id")
)
